commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

repository.name: mirror-app
repository.description: English learning app through movie scenes

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Fix debug shortcuts and remove hyperspace tunnel dithering

Debug shortcuts (comma/period/slash) now set the forced destination
type immediately on keypress, before the warp-active guard, so they
work reliably even during an active warp. Changed from hold+Space
to press-once-to-set model.

Removed hash-noise dithering from hyperspace tunnel rings — the
step() pattern created visible dots and bands across the screen.
Rings now use smooth intensity blending instead.

- Exercices/méthodes/apprendre: description + openGraph dans generateMetadata
- Page not-found.tsx globale avec i18n FR/EN/ES
- SearchModal: role=dialog + aria-modal
- Enseignant close buttons: aria-label
- Carnet remove button: aria-label

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: performance, flight trails, globe occlusion, selection UX, POV fix

Performance:
- Aircraft limited to N America + Europe bbox (~300 vs 7000+ global)
- Satellites capped at 100 (was 180)
- disableDepthTestDistance: 1.5e6 on all entities — entities on far side
  of the globe are now properly occluded by the globe (not visible through it)
- Vehicle particles only appear within 50km of camera

Flight patterns:
- Aircraft maintain last 4 positions in positionHistory map
- Shader canvas projects history to screen-space each frame and draws
  fading yellow trail lines (opacity increases toward most recent point)

Globe occlusion fix:
- Replaced Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY with 1.5e6 m threshold
  Entities within 1500km of camera: depth test disabled (prevent terrain clip)
  Entities farther (other side of globe): depth tested and correctly hidden

Selection UX:
- Clicking an entity automatically starts tracking (viewer.trackedEntity)
- Clicking empty space or pressing Escape stops tracking and closes panel
- No separate TRACK button — selection IS tracking
- Orange pulsing targeting brackets drawn on shader canvas at entity position
  (circle + 4 corner brackets, glow shadow, pulsing radius)
- HUD title pulses orange with "TRACKING: <name>"

Satellite orbital track:
- Selecting a satellite draws its next 100 min orbital ground track
  as a dashed cyan polyline; clears automatically on deselect

POV:
- Opens at 14,000km straight down (-90° pitch) — full Earth visible
- Flies to 1,200km, -82° pitch in 4s (nearly straight down, slight tilt)
- Camera constraints: min 300km, max 25,000km altitude

commit.message:
fix(ci): prevent npm symlinks from corrupting Wisp source during postinstall

npm ci creates symlinks for file: deps, so rm -rf in postinstall
follows the symlink and deletes actual source files. Fix by using
--ignore-scripts, cleaning symlinks, then running postinstall manually.

El error 'guardado con campos mínimos (columnas SP incompletas): cierres'

commit.message:
feat: implement CIPA/CCPA-compliant cookie consent system

- Add CookieConsentBanner with Accept/Decline buttons; persists choice
  to localStorage (cookie_consent: granted | denied); re-openable via
  custom 'openCookiePreferences' event
- Add GoogleAnalytics component gated on consent; listens for
  cookieConsentUpdated event to load/unload GA4 without page reload
- Add ManageCookiesLink inline client component for privacy policy page

[DIET-224] Add export_wiring_yaml command with artifact integrity and observability (#229)

- New management command: export_wiring_yaml <plan_id> --output PATH
- Validates plan has completed generation (status=generated) before export
- Writes YAML atomically via temp-file + os.replace() to prevent partial files
- Re-reads and re-parses written file to verify write integrity
- Emits integrity metadata: sha256, bytes, lines, document count, plan_id, timestamp
- Rerun on unchanged inventory yields identical sha256 (reproducible)

Tests (9, all pass):
- Happy path: file written, parses as complete YAML stream
- Metadata: sha256/bytes/lines/documents all emitted, sha256 matches file content
- Minimum CRD kinds: VLANNamespace + IPv4Namespace always present
- Reproducibility: identical sha256 on rerun with unchanged inventory
- Precondition failures: non-existent plan, missing generation state, failed status
- Invalid path: non-existent output directory raises CommandError

## Invariants
- Unchanged: generation semantics, yaml_generator output, existing export view
- Changed: artifact capture reliability and observability only

commit.message:
fix(opencode-parser): accept malformed NDJSON as plaintext fallback (#1402)

When OpenCode CLI outputs error messages or unrecognized NDJSON events,
the parser now distinguishes between:
- Recognized NDJSON with no text content → return null (correct behavior)
- Unrecognized/malformed NDJSON → accept as plaintext (new behavior)

Adds isRecognizedLegacyEvent() to track whether any known event types
were found, preventing false plaintext acceptance of valid empty streams
while recovering content from OpenCode error output.

commit.message:
feat(repo): redesign early-return analyzer with 3 orthogonal patterns

Replace old guard-based detection (missing-guard, has-guard) with 3 actionable
patterns that directly measure nesting reduction:

- wrapping-if: block's last if (no else) wraps remaining code
- invertible-if-else: imbalanced if-else where short side exits
- cascade-guard: else-if chain flattenable to sequential guards

All 3 work in both function bodies (return/throw) and loop bodies
(continue/break). Score: Σ(depthReduction × statementsAffected), threshold ≥ 2.

Add EARLY_RETURN.md documenting the remaining implicit-else pattern.

feat(skill): add openclaw-cli skill — query_openclaw MCP tool + workspace skill

Layer 1 — arifOS MCP (aaa_mcp/):
- New tool: query_openclaw(session_id, action='health'|'status')
- Integration: aaa_mcp/integrations/openclaw_gateway_client.py
  - HTTP /healthz probe (reachable from arifosmcp_server container via internal DNS)
  - docker inspect openclaw_gateway for container state
  - openclaw.json config snapshot (model, bind, version — no secrets)
  - Architecture note: OpenClaw API is WebSocket-only (custom protocol);
    HTTP probe is the correct observable subset without a WS client

Layer 2 — OpenClaw workspace skill (committed separately to openclaw-workspace):
- skills/openclaw-cli/SKILL.md + openclaw_cli_skill.py
- Full CLI access (health/status/models/channels/memory search)
- Runs inside openclaw_gateway container where `openclaw` binary exists

Layer 3 — 333_APPS/L2_SKILLS/openclaw-cli/SKILL.md
- Documents both layers, 888_HOLD boundary, and architecture
